title: Majestic sweetness sits enthroned
publicDomainBasis: Samuel Stennett (1727–1795), Thomas Hastings (1784–1872). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Majestic sweetness sits enthroned
Upon the Savior’s brow;
His head with radiant glories crowned,
His lips with grace o’erflow.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
No mortal can with Him compare
Among the sons of men;
Fairer is He than all the fair
That fill the heavenly train.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
To Him I owe my life and breath,
And all the joys I have;
He makes me triumph over death,
And saves me from the grave.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
To God, the Father, my abode,
He brings my weary feet;
Shows me the glories of my God,
And makes my joys complete.

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
Since from His bounty I receive
Such proofs of love divine,
Had I a thousand hearts to give,
Lord, they should all be Thine.
